title: Fix camera access in CameraPreview (v5 attempt)

requirements.summary: Rewrite camera toggle logic in CameraPreview to trigger native browser permission popup and display blurred video feed
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the camera toggle button triggers the browser's native camera permission popup on first use.
After granting permission, the blurred video feed appears in the participant tile.
The blur filter is always applied and cannot be removed by any UI action.
If the user denies permission, a clear error message is shown inside the CameraPreview component.
No `enumerateDevices()` call or any async pre-check precedes the `getUserMedia` call.
The media stream is fully stopped and released when the component unmounts or the camera is toggled off.
The component works correctly across Chrome, Firefox, and Safari.

file_operations.description: Rewrite the camera toggle logic from scratch. Remove all legacy code paths (enumerateDevices checks, overconstrained retries, deviceId constraints). Implement a simple camera handler that: (1) stops any existing media stream tracks first; (2) immediately calls `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ video: true, audio: false })` with no prior async calls or feature-detection guards; (3) on success, assigns the stream to `videoRef.current.srcObject` and calls `videoRef.current.play()`; (4) on failure, catches the error, sets an error state string, and displays a human-readable message ('Camera access was denied. Please allow camera access in your browser settings and try again.'). Apply a permanent CSS blur filter (`blur(12px)` with brightness/contrast adjustments) to the `<video>` element via inline style or a dedicated CSS class that is never conditionally removed. Ensure proper cleanup of media streams (stop all tracks) on component unmount using a useEffect cleanup function. Verify the camera component's usage instructions before implementing.
